function.name: revert
function.description: Undo the last reversible tool call. Works for: create_vm, clone_vm, launch_vm, stop_vm, update_config, create_profile, snapshot_create, create_network. Does not work after delete_vm, delete_profile, delete_network, snapshot_delete, snapshot_restore, or resize_disk (those are irreversible). Call this when the user says 'undo', 'revert', or 'undo that'.

function.name: clarify
function.description: Ask the user for a specific missing required piece of information. Only use for truly required fields that cannot be defaulted. Ask ONE thing at a time.

function.name: check_system
function.description: Check host system capabilities: KVM, OVMF, QEMU, CPU, RAM, disk space.

function.name: scan_isos
function.description: Scan common directories for ISO files the user might want to use.

function.name: list_vms
function.description: List all VMs with status. Optional 'label' filters to VMs carrying that flag or user label (e.g. 'work_vm', 'stealth').

label.description: Only list VMs with this flag or user label.

function.name: add_label
function.description: Add a user-defined label/tag (e.g. 'work_vm', 'test_vm') to a VM. The label is registered machine-wide so it can be reused across VMs. Works whether the VM is running or stopped.

function.name: remove_label
function.description: Remove a user-defined label/tag from a VM. The label stays in the machine-wide registry for other VMs.

function.name: list_labels
function.description: List every user-defined label registered on the machine and which VMs carry each.

function.name: list_profiles
function.description: List all hardware profiles including custom ones.

function.name: check_profile_compatibility
function.description: Check if a hardware profile is compatible with this system.

function.name: create_profile
function.description: Create or overwrite a hardware profile template (a reusable blueprint stored under a profile name). Use this ONLY for managing profile templates — never for changing a specific VM's settings. To change a VM's memory, CPU, or other config fields, use update_config instead.

force.description: Skip pre-flight checks and save the profile as-is. Only set this if the user explicitly confirmed they want to proceed despite warnings.

function.name: delete_profile
function.description: Delete a custom hardware profile.

function.name: create_vm
function.description: Create a new VM. Use clarify if name is missing. All other fields have defaults.

os_type.description: OS family: 'linux', 'windows', 'macos', or 'other'. If user names a distro (mint, ubuntu, kali…) set to 'linux'. Only clarify if completely unknown.

os_name.description: Specific distro or OS name the user mentioned (e.g. 'mint', 'ubuntu', 'kali', 'fedora'). Always set this when the user names a distro — the system uses it to auto-find the matching ISO.

disk_model.description: Disk model string exposed to the guest OS via inxi -D. E.g. 'Samsung SSD 870 EVO', 'WDC WD10EZEX-08WN4A0', 'ST1000LM049-2GH172', 'Samsung SSD 980 PRO', 'Crucial CT500MX500SSD1', 'INTEL SSDPEKNU512GZ'. Supported on sata, nvme, scsi, and ide buses. Not available on virtio.

chassis_type.description: SMBIOS chassis type string, e.g. 'Notebook', 'Desktop', 'Tower'. Maps to -smbios type=3.

hardened.description: Enable hardened mode: hides hypervisor CPUID, QEMU seccomp sandbox, forces NAT, disables balloon. Use for security isolation of untrusted guests.

stealth.description: Enable stealth mode (implies hardened). Applies full hardware fingerprint masking so the VM passes inxi -F as bare-metal hardware: SMBIOS chassis/board/BIOS overrides, PCI vendor spoofing (cirrus-vga, nec-usb-xhci), ACPI OEM ID, CPU flag hiding. Also auto-generates and serves a guest setup script on first launch (Firefox WebGL spoof + qemu_fw_cfg blacklist). Use when the VM must appear as real hardware to the OS and browser fingerprinting.

overwrite.description: If true and a VM with this name already exists, delete it and recreate. Only set this when the user explicitly said to overwrite or replace the existing VM.

manual.description: Set to true when the user said 'manual' and wants to configure each VM individually during batch creation. The CLI will prompt for os_type, memory_mb, cpu_cores, and disk_size_gb before creating each VM.

labels.description: User-defined labels/tags to assign at creation (e.g. ['work_vm', 'test_vm']). Registered machine-wide so they can be reused across VMs and used to filter list_vms.

template.description: Name of a golden-image template (created via mark_as_template) to clone this VM's disk(s) from, instead of creating blank disks. Use when the user says something like 'give it the X template disk' or 'create it from the X template'. Call list_templates to see what's available.

randomize_root_password.description: When cloning from a Linux template, offline-edit the new disk's root password to a fresh random value (via virt-customize) instead of inheriting the template's exact password. Only applies with 'template' set on a Linux template; ignored for Windows. Only set when the user asks for a unique/randomized/different password per clone.

randomize_user_password.description: Same as randomize_root_password, but for the disk's primary (non-root) user account instead — the username is auto-detected from the disk (first account with UID >= 1000). Only applies with 'template' set on a Linux template; ignored for Windows. Only set when the user asks for a unique/randomized user-account password per clone.

new_username.description: Rename the cloned disk's primary (non-root) user account to this — the old username is auto-detected from the disk. Only applies with 'template' set on a Linux template; ignored for Windows. Only set when the user explicitly asks for a unique/different/renamed username per clone. Must be a valid lowercase Linux username.

unattended.description: Fully automate OS installation instead of showing the interactive installer (Windows and Linux both supported). WIPES the target disk. Only set when the user explicitly asks for unattended/automated/hands-off install.

unattended_username.description: Account username for the unattended install. Ignored if unattended_skip_user is true.

unattended_password.description: Account password for the unattended install. Ignored if unattended_skip_user is true.

unattended_locale.description: Locale for the unattended install, e.g. 'en-US'.

unattended_autologon.description: Windows only: auto-login as the created account on first boot. Ignored if unattended_skip_user is true.

unattended_skip_user.description: Automate everything (disk, locale, network/hardware-check bypass) EXCEPT the account — the installer stops at its normal 'create your account' screen for the user to fill in themselves. Use when the user says something like 'automate everything except creating the user' or 'let me create my own account'.

function.name: clone_vm
function.description: Clone an existing VM into a new VM with copy-on-write disks.

function.name: mark_as_template
function.description: Turn a stopped VM into a reusable golden-image template. Flattens its current disk state into a protected copy under ~/.qemu_vms/_templates/ and tags the VM with the protected 'template' label. Use when the user says 'mark X as a template' or similar. The VM must be stopped first.

name.description: VM to snapshot as a template.

function.name: remove_template
function.description: Permanently delete a golden-image template's disk copy and remove the 'template' label from the source VM if it still exists. This is destructive and always asks the user to confirm first — never skip the confirmation.

name.description: Template name (same as the VM it was created from).

function.name: list_templates
function.description: List every golden-image template registered on the machine.

function.name: launch_vm
function.description: Start an already-existing VM. Use this when the user says start, launch, run, or boot a VM that has already been created. Do NOT use create_vm — that creates a new VM from scratch.

function.name: stop_vm
function.description: Stop or shut down a running VM. Use name='all' to stop all VMs. Call this when the user says stop, shut down, halt, kill, or force-stop a VM. Set force=true when the user says 'kill', 'force stop', 'hard stop', or 'SIGKILL'. This does not delete anything — the VM can be relaunched afterwards.

function.name: vm_status
function.description: Get status of a VM.

function.name: monitor_vm
function.description: Deep activity report. Use name='all' for all VMs.

function.name: show_config
function.description: Show full VM configuration.

function.name: update_config
function.description: Update one or more config fields of a specific VM (memory_mb, cpu_cores, cpu_model, disk_size_gb, iso_path, network_mode, uefi, etc.). Use this whenever the user wants to change a VM's settings — e.g. 'give it more RAM', 'halve the memory', 'change the CPU'. The VM must be stopped first.

function.name: resize_disk
function.description: Resize a VM disk (VM must be stopped).

function.name: snapshot_create
function.description: Create a snapshot of a running VM.

function.name: snapshot_list
function.description: List all snapshots for a VM.

function.name: snapshot_restore
function.description: Restore a VM snapshot by name.

function.name: snapshot_delete
function.description: Delete a named snapshot from a VM. Use this when the user wants to remove or discard a specific snapshot by name. Do NOT use delete_vm — that removes the entire VM, not just a snapshot.

function.name: set_resource_limits
function.description: Limit CPU% or memory of a running VM.

function.name: create_network
function.description: Create a private isolated network between VMs (no internet access).

function.name: delete_network
function.description: Delete an isolated network.

function.name: list_networks
function.description: List all isolated VM networks.

function.name: add_vm_to_network
function.description: Add a VM to an isolated network.

function.name: open_display
function.description: Open the display viewer for a running VM.

function.name: open_shell
function.description: Open a serial console in a terminal.

function.name: delete_vm
function.description: Permanently delete a VM configuration and its disk files. Use ONLY when the user says delete, remove, destroy, or wipe a VM. Do NOT call this for 'kill' — kill means stop_vm(force=True). Do NOT use this to delete a snapshot — use snapshot_delete for that.

function.name: check_disk
function.description: Check whether a VM's disk images are blank (no OS installed). Call this when a VM boots but shows a PXE network boot screen, 'no bootable device', or TianoCore UEFI with no OS found — those symptoms mean the disk is empty, not a QEMU crash. Returns has_blank_disk, per-disk size info, and a suggested_iso auto-matched from available ISOs using the VM's os_type and os_name fields, filtered for arch compatibility. When has_blank_disk is true: use suggested_iso directly if present (do not ask the user), call update_config with that iso_path, then call launch_vm. Only ask the user if suggested_iso is null.

function.name: get_vm_logs
function.description: Read a VM launch log and diagnose why it failed. ALWAYS call this when a VM is stopped unexpectedly, fails to launch, crashes immediately, or the user asks why a VM failed. Returns parsed errors, root cause diagnosis, and fix suggestions.

function.name: print_command
function.description: Print the full QEMU launch command without running it.

function.name: fingerprint_vm
function.description: Analyse a VM's config and report what a fingerprinting tool (inxi -M -N -C -D -A -G) would see from inside the guest. Scores SMBIOS fields, MAC OUI, CPU model, disk interface, audio, and GPU for VM detectability. Call this when the user asks about VM fingerprinting, detectability, or how realistic a VM looks. Use summary=true when fingerprinting multiple VMs to skip the full report and only return the score — required when computing an average across VMs.

summary.description: Return score only, skip full report rendering. Use when fingerprinting multiple VMs or computing averages.

function.name: send_monitor_cmd
function.description: Send a raw QEMU monitor protocol command to a running VM (e.g. 'info status', 'info block', 'info kvm', 'quit'). Use this for low-level QEMU introspection or control. Do NOT use vm_status — that only returns high-level running/stopped state.
